artifacts.description: A Customer places one or more Orders. The 'orders' table typically contains a foreign key (e.g., customer_id) referencing the 'customers' table to track which individual or organization made the purchase.

evidence.summary: Represents the individuals or organizations that purchase goods or services, containing key demographic, contact, and account information.
Represents a customer purchase transaction, encompassing both the overarching order details (such as shipping and dates) and the specific line items purchased.

artifacts.name: Customer 1:N Order

payload.description: A Customer places one or more Orders. The 'orders' table typically contains a foreign key (e.g., customer_id) referencing the 'customers' table to track which individual or organization made the purchase.

artifacts.description: An Employee (such as a sales representative) processes, manages, or is credited with Orders. The 'orders' table typically contains a foreign key (e.g., employee_id) referencing the 'employees' table.

evidence.summary: Contains details about the enterprise's workforce, including personal information, job titles, departmental alignments, and reporting structures.
Represents a customer purchase transaction, encompassing both the overarching order details (such as shipping and dates) and the specific line items purchased.

artifacts.name: Employee 1:N Order

payload.description: An Employee (such as a sales representative) processes, manages, or is credited with Orders. The 'orders' table typically contains a foreign key (e.g., employee_id) referencing the 'employees' table.

artifacts.description: An Order contains multiple Products, and a Product can be sold in multiple Orders. This many-to-many relationship is resolved by the 'order_details' table, which acts as a junction table containing foreign keys to both 'orders' and 'products'.

evidence.summary: Represents a customer purchase transaction, encompassing both the overarching order details (such as shipping and dates) and the specific line items purchased.
Defines the master catalog of goods and services available for sale, including product specifications, pricing, inventory levels, and their respective classifications or categories.

artifacts.description: Represents a customer purchase transaction, encompassing both the overarching order details (such as shipping and dates) and the specific line items purchased.

raw_payload.table_comment: [Order Line Item] Captures the granular line-level specifics of a customer transaction, specifying the exact products purchased, quantities, unit prices, and applied discounts.

evidence.summary: [Order Line Item] Captures the granular line-level specifics of a customer transaction, specifying the exact products purchased, quantities, unit prices, and applied discounts.

raw_payload.table_comment: [Sales Order] Records the header-level details of customer purchase transactions, tracking order dates, fulfillment status, shipping information, and total transaction value.

evidence.summary: [Sales Order] Records the header-level details of customer purchase transactions, tracking order dates, fulfillment status, shipping information, and total transaction value.

raw_payload.table_comment: [Product Category] Defines the groupings or classifications of products offered by the enterprise, facilitating inventory management, sales reporting, and catalog navigation.

evidence.summary: [Product Category] Defines the groupings or classifications of products offered by the enterprise, facilitating inventory management, sales reporting, and catalog navigation.
